name: Employee Training Platform
professionalExpectations: A training platform must organize learning paths, modules, assessments, progress, and manager visibility.
The first version should make training content understandable and trackable without unnecessary learning-management complexity.
Access, identity, and employee data assumptions must be treated as gated until the owner approves live handling.
Quality depends on clear learning outcomes, not only polished screens.
minimumQualityBar: Learning paths, modules, completion rules, and assessment expectations are explicit.
No employee, customer, production, HR, identity, email, or paid platform data is connected without approval.
Content structure supports review, versioning, and future updates.
Progress and reporting are modeled clearly before live account or database work.
commonModules: Course catalog, learning path, module detail, lesson content, quiz or checklist, and completion tracking.
Learner progress, manager overview, content version history, and audit-ready update notes.
Role and access plan for owner, future managers, future learners, and future content editors.
Reporting model for completion, assessment, gaps, and overdue items.
uxExpectations: Learning flows should be calm, readable, and easy to resume.
Progress, requirements, and completion status must be visible without confusion.
Manager views should be compact and focused on exceptions, overdue work, and training gaps.
Assessments should be simple first, with clear pass criteria and feedback states.

components: Static or local content records for the first curriculum model
GitHub for versioned content and app source
Existing Postgres only after approved learner-data handling
Netlify only after approved staging or production deployment
Identity provider only after owner-approved access plan
recommendedStack.notes: Start with content architecture and local progress model. Live employee accounts and HR data are gated.

phases.name: Curriculum Model
goals: Define learning paths, modules, outcomes, assessments, and completion rules.
Classify learner and manager data before runtime storage exists.

phases.name: Read-Only Platform
goals: Build catalog, lesson, and progress-review views with local records.
Validate quality and accessibility of learning content.

phases.name: Approved Tracking
goals: Add identity, storage, reporting, and notifications only after approval gates pass.
Document backup, retention, and rollback requirements.

knownPitfalls: Building a full LMS before content and learning outcomes are stable.
Collecting employee or HR-related data without classification and approval.
Making progress reporting too complex for the first operating version.
Ignoring content versioning and review cycles.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before identity, email, HR, employee, customer, production, or paid service integration.
Owner approval is required before storing learner progress outside local records.
Owner approval is required before deployment, domain or DNS changes, credentials, or notifications.
Owner approval is required before using live company training materials that contain confidential or restricted data.
qualityChecklist: Learning outcomes, modules, completion, and assessments are clear.
Learner and manager views are accessible, responsive, and easy to scan.
Data classification, retention, backup, and rollback needs are documented.
No live identity, HR, notification, deployment, or paid action is present without approval.
whatToBuildFirst: Completion and assessment state model using local records.
Manager reporting wireframe for completion and gaps.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Full identity and permissions before the learning model is validated.
Advanced certificates, gamification, or recommendation engines.
Complex analytics before basic completion and assessment quality are useful.
Live notifications or HR integrations before approval and data handling rules.
stopConditions: Stop before handling employee, HR, customer, production, confidential, or restricted data.
Stop before connecting identity, email, notifications, database, deployment, domain, DNS, paid services, or credentials.
Stop before presenting training records as official compliance evidence without owner-approved requirements.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not authorize employee-data handling or live training deployment.
